chunks.title: Introduction
chunks.content: This document provides essential information for healthcare professionals and patients regarding asthma management, focusing on inhaled corticosteroids and long-acting beta-agonists.

chunks.title: Medications - Inhaled Corticosteroids (ICS)
chunks.content: ICSs are the cornerstone of asthma treatment for long-term control. They reduce inflammation in the airways and are taken daily.

chunks.title: Medications - Long-Acting Beta2-Agonists (LABA)
chunks.content: LABAs are used in combination with ICS for patients who require additional asthma control. They should not be used alone.

chunks.title: Patient Education - Common Questions
chunks.content: Addressing patient concerns about side effects, growth, and safety of ICSs and LABAs.

chunks.title: Patient Education - Proper Use
chunks.content: Emphasizing correct inhaler technique and the importance of rinsing the mouth after ICS use.

chunks.title: Resources
chunks.content: A list of organizations and websites for further information on asthma management.
